channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: UN Declares West Asia Crisis a Humanitarian Emergency Amid Israel-Iran Conflict
item.description: On March 6, 2026, the conflict between Israel and Iran intensified with Israeli airstrikes targeting the capitals of Iran and Lebanon. The U.S. reportedly struck an Iranian drone carrier at sea, escal...

item.title: U.S. Military Presence in Gulf States Increases Regional Security Risks
item.description: Before recent U.S.-Iran tensions escalated, Gulf countries believed hosting American military bases would strengthen their security by deterring attacks. However, recent events have shown that these b...

item.title: Critique of Western Support for US-Israel Strikes on Iran in 2026
item.description: In this opinion article dated March 3, 2026, Dr. Binoy Kampmark argues that US allies have abandoned international law principles by endorsing or tolerating the joint US-Israeli military strikes on Ir...

item.title: Punjab to deploy specialised units for drone surveillance and neutralisation
item.description: The Punjab government has decided to establish anti-drone units in every district of the province to tackle unauthorised drones operated by enemies of Pakistan or miscreants. According to the Home Dep...

item.title: Councils call for review of court fines for fly-tipping as they fall below fixed penalties and incidents rise
item.description: Councils in England are urging the government and the Sentencing Council to review sentencing guidelines for fly-tipping offences, arguing that fines imposed by courts are often lower than those issue...

item.title: Experts Warn About Health Risks of Unregulated Peptides Promoted Online
item.description: Peptides, short chains of amino acids that can act as cell messengers, have gained popularity online thanks to influencers promoting them for cosmetic, fitness, and health benefits. In Australia, infl...

item.title: Kenyan Police Clarify They Are Not Preparing to Fight Foreign Countries
item.description: Recently, there were posts circulating on Facebook claiming that the Kenyan police had stated they were ready to defend Kenya from potential attacks by the US, Israel, and Iran amid the ongoing Middle...

item.title: Stakeholders Discuss Public Access to CVFF Application Portal
item.description: Since the launch of the Cabotage Vessel Financing Fund (CVFF) Application Portal by the Honourable Minister of Marine and Blue Economy, Dr. Adegboyega Oyetola, many stakeholders don dey confused about...

item.title: Tanzania Strengthens International Cooperation to Advance Climate and Environmental Goals
item.description: Tanzania imeendelea kuthibitisha nia yake ya kushirikiana na wadau wa maendeleo duniani ili kuharakisha utekelezaji wa vipaumbele vya taifa vya uhifadhi wa mazingira na mabadiliko ya tabianchi. Hii il...

item.title: The Physics Behind Squeak-Free Sports Shoes
item.description: The high-pitched squeak of sports shoes is a familiar background sound in basketball, squash, and tennis, but scientists are now exploring whether this can be eliminated without sacrificing grip. Rese...

item.title: South Africa braces for economic impact of Middle East conflict
item.description: The recent US–Israel military action against Iran is triggering instability in global energy markets, with direct consequences for South Africa's economy. Finance Minister Enoch Godongwana has warned ...

item.title: Federal High Court orders freeze on Petrocam accounts over disputed N9.05 billion Zenith Bank loan
item.description: The Federal High Court for Lagos don order say make all accounts wey relate to Petrocam Trading Nigeria Limited and the company oga, Patrick Ilo, no fit touch for now over N9.05 billion debt wey Zenit...

item.title: Toronto lawyer denied reinstatement after links to mortgage fraud connected to 2024 triple homicide
item.description: Shahryar Mazaheri, a Toronto lawyer, has failed to have his licence reinstated following an interlocutory suspension imposed in November 2024 due to his involvement in a mortgage fraud scheme. The sch...

item.title: US Justice Department Releases Previously Withheld Epstein Records Mentioning Trump
item.description: On Thursday, the US Justice Department made public certain FBI records that had previously been unavailable, relating to the late financier Jeffrey Epstein. These records include summaries of intervie...

item.title: How Plantain Chips Can Be Unsafe If Fried Wrong Way
item.description: For many Nigerians and West Africans, plantain chips na daily snack wey you fit see everywhere—roadside stalls, small kiosks, and even supermarkets. But wetin most people no sabi be say the way some v...

item.title: Protecting Journalists’ Safety Must Be Priority, Not Afterthought
item.description: Dis na one heavy matter wey don happen for Nigeria journalism circle. One journalist, Kani Ben from Channels Television, don die after road accident while covering official trip for Bauchi State. D ar...

item.title: Nigeria Settles 15-Year Oil Dispute as African Countries Face Health, Governance, and Sports Challenges
item.description: On 6th March 2026, Nigeria achieved a major breakthrough by resolving the long-standing dispute over Oil Prospecting Licence 245 after 15 years of legal wrangles. President Bola Tinubu led the settlem...

item.title: California’s Sierra Nevada Snowpack Declines Rapidly Due to Early Spring Heat Wave
item.description: In late February and early March 2026, California experienced a dramatic reduction in its Sierra Nevada snowpack following an intense winter heat wave. NASA satellite imagery reveals that snow levels ...

item.title: Nicola Peltz Focuses on Work Amid Family Dispute Involving Brooklyn Beckham
item.description: Nicola Peltz, 31, addressed questions about the ongoing public dispute between her husband Brooklyn Beckham, 27, and his parents, David and Victoria Beckham, by emphasising her focus on work rather th...

item.title: Paul Keating Critiques Nine Newspapers’ Alarmist China Coverage
item.description: On the third anniversary of Nine newspapers’ 2023 'Red Alert' series, former Prime Minister Paul Keating has offered a pointed critique. The series, authored by Peter Hartcher and Matthew Knott, warne...
